behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real speech model’s diarization quality, not just wiring. A mock/stub STT response would hide label-loss or speaker-confusion failures. Real external API use suggests System, but behavior correctness depends on model output content (speaker labeling accuracy), so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior targets one model-output property (speaker labels), not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for attendees in enough turns to support downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/transform logic over transcript text into a structured object. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required, and LLM/STT output quality is not required if explicit assignments are provided in fixtures. Rejected Integration: no required cross-module failure is implied by the behavior text beyond local extraction logic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignments (e.g., 'Alice will send the budget') into the parsing function and assert extracted assignee/task pairs in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summarization behavior (coverage/completeness per assignee). Deterministic mocks can always emit perfect coverage and hide real omission failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be validated with a transcript containing known assignments and one real summarization step; it does not require proving correctness of multiple agentic handoff stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript (or STT output) with multiple explicit assignees to the real summarizer model and assert final action items include at least one item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is schema-routing logic for already identified decisions. The failure is local mapping/placement logic, testable without real infra or model output quality. Rejected Integration: behavior text scopes to routing of identified decisions, not module handoff correctness across system boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing function with fixtures containing pre-identified decision objects and assert they populate `decisions` fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model extraction and placement quality from natural-language transcript content. Mocked model output would hide missed/incorrect decision capture. Rejected Workflow: although the pipeline has stages, this behavior’s failure can be observed at the summarizer decision quality boundary without requiring validation of multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summarization on transcripts with known decision statements and assert those decisions appear in structured `decisions` output rather than only free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior inherently spans multiple sequential processing steps (segmenting, repeated model calls, merge coherence). It includes multi-step agentic dependence: outputs from earlier segment-level model calls affect later merge quality. Lower levels miss cross-step degradation and coherence failures. Rejected Agentic: a single-call agentic test cannot validate the sequential multi-step orchestration and merge behavior required by the text.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting audio through the end-to-end segmented pipeline, verify sequential segment processing occurred, and assert merged final summary is structurally complete and coherent across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end multi-stage orchestration with real recording, real STT, and real LLM summarization. It requires validating chained agentic steps and system boundaries together. Any lower-level isolated test can pass while real pipeline handoffs fail.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run using a real meeting recording from upload through final structured delivery, asserting automatic progression through all stages and valid final schema output without human intervention.
